_note: Backup of the alkatera-lca-verifier Supabase project (ref goriowvxkvmizwtenpju) before deletion to free a project slot for alkatera staging. Only the reference data is captured; the app's other tables (verifications, findings, calculation_checks) were empty, and profiles/badges/payments held 1 throwaway row each. Restore by inserting these into a fresh verifier project's standards / standard_clauses tables (standard_clauses.standard_id references standards.id).

standards.name: ISO 14040:2006
standards.description: Environmental management. Life cycle assessment. Principles and framework.

standards.name: ISO 14044:2006
standards.description: Environmental management. Life cycle assessment. Requirements and guidelines.

standards.name: ISO 14067:2018
standards.description: Greenhouse gases. Carbon footprint of products. Requirements and guidelines for quantification.

standards.name: ISO 14046:2014
standards.description: Environmental management. Water footprint. Principles, requirements and guidelines.

standards.name: GHG Protocol Product Standard
standards.description: Product Life Cycle Accounting and Reporting Standard (WRI/WBCSD).

standards.name: PAS 2050:2011
standards.description: Specification for the assessment of the life cycle greenhouse gas emissions of goods and services.

standards.name: Science Based Targets initiative
standards.description: Corporate target-setting framework; product LCAs feed scope 3 inventories and product-level targets.

standards.name: CDP Climate Disclosure
standards.description: Disclosure framework; product footprints support corporate climate reporting.

standards.name: EU Green Claims Directive
standards.description: Substantiation requirements for explicit environmental claims made to EU consumers.

standard_clauses.title: Goal and scope definition
standard_clauses.check_description: The study explicitly states its goal (intended application, reasons, audience, whether comparative assertions are disclosed to the public) and scope, including the functional unit and system boundary with justified cut-off criteria.

standard_clauses.title: Data quality requirements
standard_clauses.check_description: Data quality requirements are specified and addressed, covering time-related, geographical and technology coverage, precision, completeness, representativeness, consistency, reproducibility, data sources, and uncertainty of the information.

standard_clauses.title: Allocation
standard_clauses.check_description: Allocation procedures are documented and justified. Wherever possible allocation is avoided through subdivision or system expansion; where unavoidable, the basis (physical relationship or other) is stated and applied consistently.

standard_clauses.title: Life cycle impact assessment
standard_clauses.check_description: LCIA methodology, impact categories, category indicators and characterisation models are documented and consistent with the goal and scope; impact category coverage is sufficient for the stated goal.

standard_clauses.title: Life cycle interpretation
standard_clauses.check_description: Interpretation identifies significant issues from the LCI and LCIA results and states conclusions, limitations and recommendations consistent with the goal and scope.

standard_clauses.title: Evaluation: completeness, sensitivity and uncertainty
standard_clauses.check_description: The interpretation includes an evaluation with completeness, sensitivity and consistency checks, and addresses uncertainty in the results (formal uncertainty and sensitivity analysis where conclusions depend on it).

standard_clauses.title: Biogenic carbon emissions and removals
standard_clauses.check_description: Biogenic carbon emissions and removals are quantified and documented separately from fossil GHG emissions and removals in the carbon footprint result.

standard_clauses.title: Scope 3 boundary compatibility
standard_clauses.check_description: The product footprint uses GHG Protocol-consistent lifecycle boundaries, recognised GWP characterisation factors, and documented data sources, such that it can credibly feed a corporate scope 3 inventory or product-level science-based target.

standard_clauses.title: Value chain emissions disclosure readiness
standard_clauses.check_description: The methodology, emission factor sources and exclusions are documented to a standard compatible with CDP value-chain disclosure: boundaries stated, exclusions justified, and calculation approach reproducible.

standard_clauses.title: Substantiation of explicit environmental claims
standard_clauses.check_description: Any explicit environmental claim resting on this study must be substantiated by recognised scientific evidence: a life-cycle perspective, significant impacts covered (no cherry-picking), primary/secondary data reliance disclosed, and trade-offs between impacts not concealed.

standard_clauses.title: Communication of claims within substantiated scope
standard_clauses.check_description: Claims must be communicated only for what was substantiated: the covered impacts, boundary and units stated alongside the claim, and the verification/review status of the underlying study disclosed rather than implied.
